title: V1.32 P3 — Close SEC-V131-01 capability identity boundary gap

fixes_during_review: rev1: 1 Critical C-001 (StateCompositeTask/CapabilityTask did not inject trusted _creator_id/_session_id into args — legitimate internal callers fell to 'default')
rev2 (targeted): 0 findings, final Approve. Re-checked by same qc-specialist who found C-001, modified qc1 report in place per PM rule.

qa_summary: judge.llm and context.summarize now read only context-injected _creator_id/_session_id (mirror acp.prompt pattern). StateCompositeTask::run() injects trusted identity at the task boundary so internal callers (orchestration engine) receive correct identity. 6 regression tests (3 per capability × 2 capabilities): raw spoof blocked, context-injected trusted, context wins on mixed input.

closed_residuals.title: judge.llm and context.summarize still read raw creator_id/session_id — same IDOR pattern as fixed acp_prompt
closed_residuals.closure_note: Both capabilities now read only context-injected _creator_id/_session_id. Orchestration engine task boundary (StateCompositeTask::run) injects trusted context at invocation. 6 regression tests prove spoofing impossible. Defense-in-depth restored. No behavior change for legitimate (context-injected) callers. See plan 2026-06-03-v1.32-orchestration-security-followup and qc reports.

notes: SEC-V131-01 closed. Pattern mirrors hardened acp.prompt. Caller-side fix was the critical addition; raw-args parsing in the capabilities was already correct in rev1 but the engine never populated the context.
